name: ethereum
context: Ethereum blockchain ecosystem — smart contracts, EVM execution, onchain payments, and developer tooling
info: The Ethereum ecosystem covers smart contract development and deployment across EVM-compatible chains (Ethereum mainnet, Base, Arbitrum, Optimism, Polygon), onchain payment protocols like x402, gas management, and wallet operations. This tree is organized around the jobs an agent actually performs: deploying contracts, accepting or sending payments, estimating gas, and managing keys. Solidity is the primary contract language. Toolchains include Hardhat and Foundry. The x402 standard enables HTTP-native stablecoin payments that any API or agent can use without accounts or credit cards.
body:
# Ethereum Ecosystem

Ethereum is a programmable blockchain where agents deploy and interact with smart contracts, send transactions, and integrate onchain payment protocols. The EVM (Ethereum Virtual Machine) runs across mainnet and L2 networks like Base, Arbitrum, Optimism, and Polygon.

## Agent Jobs

The primary tasks an agent performs in this ecosystem:

- **Deploy contracts** — compile Solidity, deploy to a target chain, verify source on a block explorer. See [[ethereum/contract-deploy]].
- **Accept payments** — serve an HTTP endpoint that returns 402 and settles stablecoin payments via x402. See [[ethereum/x402/server]].
- **Send payments** — programmatically pay for APIs and resources using x402. See [[ethereum/x402/client]].
- **Estimate gas** — predict transaction costs and set appropriate gas parameters. See [[ethereum/gas]].
- **Manage wallets** — generate keys, sign transactions, use hardware or cloud signers. See [[ethereum/wallets]].

## Chains

All skills in this tree work across EVM-compatible chains. Chain identifiers follow CAIP-2 format:

| Chain | CAIP-2 ID | Common Use |
|-------|-----------|------------|
| Ethereum Mainnet | eip155:1 | High-value contracts, DeFi |
| Base | eip155:8453 | x402 payments, low-cost deploys |
| Base Sepolia | eip155:84532 | Testnet for Base |
| Arbitrum One | eip155:42161 | DeFi, low fees |
| Optimism | eip155:10 | Governance, public goods |
| Polygon | eip155:137 | High throughput, low fees |

## Libraries

- **ethers.js v6** — general-purpose Ethereum library for JS/TS
- **viem** — TypeScript-first, type-safe alternative to ethers
- **web3.py** — Python Ethereum library
- **Hardhat** — development environment, testing, deployment
- **Foundry** — Rust-based toolchain with forge, cast, anvil

sections.title: Supported chains
sections.body: All skills target EVM-compatible chains. Use CAIP-2 identifiers: `eip155:1` (mainnet), `eip155:8453` (Base), `eip155:84532` (Base Sepolia), `eip155:42161` (Arbitrum), `eip155:10` (Optimism), `eip155:137` (Polygon). L2s are preferred for low-cost deployments and x402 payments.

sections.title: Core libraries
sections.body: ethers.js v6 and viem are the primary JS/TS libraries. web3.py for Python. Hardhat and Foundry are the two main development toolchains. The x402 SDK wraps payment operations across `@x402/core`, `@x402/express`, `@x402/fetch`, and `@x402/axios`.

sections.title: Getting started
sections.body: To deploy contracts, start with [[ethereum/contract-deploy]]. To integrate payments, start with [[ethereum/x402]]. For gas estimation, see [[ethereum/gas]]. For wallet setup, see [[ethereum/wallets]].
